entity.summary: Cryptocurrency exchange launched after the BTC-e seizure and widely described as a successor platform for BTC-e users. WEX stopped functioning in late 2018 amid withdrawal failures, missing customer funds, and later law-enforcement actions involving former WEX leadership.
entity.notes: HEI treats WEX as a separate entity from BTC-e because it operated under a distinct WEX brand and domain after BTC-e's 2017 seizure. It is not linked with successor_id because the relationship is operationally and legally ambiguous. death_reason is scam_rug because available sources describe missing customer funds and fraud-related law-enforcement actions after the exchange stopped operating, but confidence remains medium because public sources do not provide a clean formal shutdown notice.

events.title: WEX launched after BTC-e seizure
events.description: WEX emerged after the 2017 BTC-e enforcement action and was widely described as a successor service through which BTC-e customer accounts or balances moved to a new exchange environment.
events.notes: Date is month-level and stored as 2017-09-01 for schema consistency. The entity is separate from the canonical BTC-e record.

events.title: WEX stopped operating with customer funds unresolved
events.description: Public references describe WEX as ceasing work in late 2018 without returning customer assets, with later reporting and legal action referring to missing funds from WEX accounts.
events.notes: Approximate month-level terminal marker. Used because available public references point to late-2018 failure rather than a precise official shutdown notice.

events.title: Former WEX head arrested in Poland
events.description: Polish authorities arrested Dmitry Vasilyev, described in reporting as a former WEX head, on suspicion of fraud and money laundering tied to the exchange's operations and missing funds.
events.notes: Post-closure legal context. This supports the fraud-centered collapse classification but is not used as the operational death date.

evidence.title: BTC-e historical reference including WEX successor context
evidence.notes: Secondary background reference describing BTC-e customer accounts moving to WEX and WEX later ceasing work without returning client assets. Used only as background due to source type.

evidence.title: Who is Alexander Vinnik, the Russian prisoner being traded for American Marc Fogel?
evidence.notes: Reuters background on BTC-e and Alexander Vinnik, used to support the BTC-e enforcement context preceding the WEX successor environment.

evidence.title: BTC-e historical reference including WEX customer-funds failure context
evidence.notes: Secondary background reference stating WEX stopped working in late 2018 without returning client assets. Used cautiously as terminal-failure context.

evidence.title: Polish police arrest former head of Russian cryptocurrency exchange WEX
evidence.notes: Reports that former WEX head Dmitry Vasilyev was arrested in Poland on fraud and money-laundering suspicions, and cites the disappearance of funds from WEX accounts in 2018.

evidence.title: WEX original domain archive
evidence.notes: Archive-first access for preserving the original WEX domain as historical evidence.
